expectations.text: Profile check (test-admin-profile.sh) happens before any install command
expectations.evidence: The transcript has no mention of test-admin-profile.sh or any profile check step. It goes directly to checking if ripgrep is installed.
The transcript header states 'Platform: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 on Windows 11 Pro (WOPR3)' and notes reference workspace preferences, indicating awareness of the WSL environment.

expectations.text: Uses apt as the package manager (profile preference), not snap/brew/other
expectations.evidence: Step 4 uses 'sudo apt install -y ripgrep'. Notes explicitly state: 'Used apt as the package manager per workspace preferences (apt over snap). No snap, cargo, or other install methods attempted.'
There is no mention of log_admin_event or any logging step anywhere in the transcript. The mandatory logging requirement from CLAUDE.md was not followed.

expectations.text: Checks if ripgrep is already installed before attempting install
expectations.evidence: Step 1 explicitly checks with 'which rg', 'rg --version', and 'dpkg -l ripgrep' before proceeding to install. The expected outcome notes that rg is currently available via Claude Code's bundled binary but no apt package is installed.
